description: Fix the catch block in get-page-views.ts incorrectly converting JSON parse errors (SyntaxError) to ToolError.notFound, giving a misleading 'page not found' error when the Wikimedia API returns malformed JSON.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/wikipedia && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Distinguish JSON parse errors from notFound in get-page-views.ts catch block
userStories.description: In plugins/wikipedia/src/tools/get-page-views.ts, the catch block at lines 44-47 converts any non-ToolError exception to ToolError.notFound, including SyntaxError from response.json() on malformed responses. When the Wikimedia API returns a malformed JSON body, the AI receives 'Page view data not found' which is incorrect — the article exists but the API returned bad data. Map SyntaxError to ToolError.internal and keep the notFound mapping only for the genuine not-found case.
acceptanceCriteria: SyntaxError from response.json() is caught and thrown as ToolError.internal (not notFound)
ToolError instances are still re-thrown as-is (ToolError.notFound from fetchFromPage on 404 propagates correctly)
Network errors that are not ToolError are thrown as ToolError.internal
